title: DNS Resolution Failure for Internal Service Discovery
symptoms: DNS resolution failures for all *.internal.company.com hostnames
All microservices logging NXDOMAIN errors for internal service names
Service mesh health checks failing across all namespaces
Route 53 private hosted zone showing no VPC associations
External DNS resolution working normally (issue isolated to internal)
root_cause: Automated infrastructure cleanup script accidentally deleted the Route 53 private hosted zone VPC association, disabling internal DNS resolution
resolution: Re-associated the Route 53 private hosted zone with the production VPC, flushed DNS caches on all service nodes, and restarted affected services to clear stale DNS entries
preventive_actions: Add deletion protection on Route 53 hosted zone associations
Implement dry-run mode for all infrastructure cleanup scripts
Create DNS resolution canary monitoring with 30-second intervals
Add IAM guardrails preventing hosted zone association deletion
Implement service mesh fallback to IP-based routing during DNS failures

timeline.event: Internal DNS resolution stops working across all services
Cascading alerts fire for service health check failures
Maria Garcia begins investigation, confirms external DNS working
Route 53 console shows no VPC association on private hosted zone
Cleanup script execution log reviewed, accidental deletion confirmed
Hosted zone re-associated with production VPC
DNS caches flushed, services beginning to recover
All services confirmed operational, DNS resolution normal

embedding_summary: Complete internal DNS resolution failure caused by automated cleanup script accidentally deleting Route 53 private hosted zone VPC association. All inter-service communication ceased for 25 minutes affecting 68,000 users. Resolved by re-associating the hosted zone and flushing DNS caches.
